claim_bullets.bullet_text: The const keyword introduces one or more bindings in the block that encloses it, without incurring the cost of a function invocation (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.51-53).
const statements occur inside blocks, and cannot be used when writing a fat arrow that has an expression as its body (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.51-53).
const allows binding any expression, including functions, to names, enabling helper functions to be bound in a readable way (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.51-53).
Multiple bindings can be declared in a single const statement by separating them with commas, with one binding per line recommended for readability (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.51-53).

source_record_text: The const keyword in JavaScript provides a way to bind values to names in a block-scoped manner. This chunk discusses the const keyword, its usage in binding values and functions, and its advantages over other approaches.
